title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence
description: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a layered packet-flow diagnosis while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use OSI and TCP/IP models as evidence maps rather than protocol mythology.
Trace addressing, encapsulation, PDUs, state, and device responsibility end to end.
Interpret packet evidence without claiming visibility into encrypted or absent layers.
